title: Two-step accelerating freezing protocol yields a better motility, membranes and DNA integrities of thawed ram sperm than three-steps freezing protocols
author: Galarza, Diego A.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
A paper produced in the frame of the H2020 project 677353 'Innovative Management of Animal GEnetic resources' 

 This study compares three protocols for freezing ram semen, differing by the cooling rate used. Sperm quality was better for the protocol including only two freezing steps, with a cooling rate of 60 °C/min in the second step,  than protocols with lower (and/or decelerating) cooling rates.
